title: Infosys board approves ₹ 18,000 Crore equity share buyback
detailed_summary: Infosys Limited's Board of Directors approved a proposal on September 11, 2025 to buy back up to 10,00,00,000 fully paid-up equity shares of face value ₹ 5/- each, representing up to 2.41% of the existing total paid-up equity share capital on a standalone basis. The buyback size is ₹ 18,000 Crore at a buyback price of ₹ 1,800/- per equity share, payable in cash, and will be conducted through the tender offer route from shareholders as of a record date to be announced later. The buyback remains subject to shareholder approval by remote e-voting and other applicable statutory approvals. Infosys also noted it will seek exemptive relief from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission due to conflicting Indian and U.S. tender offer requirements, and ADS holders may participate if they cancel ADSs and withdraw the underlying equity shares by the record date.

key_facts.fact: The Board of Directors approved a proposal to buy back equity shares for an amount of ₹ 18,000 Crore at a price of ₹ 1,800/- per Equity Share, payable in cash.

The buyback comprises purchase of 10,00,00,000 fully paid-up equity shares of face value of ₹ 5/- each, representing up to 2.41% of the total number of Equity Shares in the existing total paid-up Equity Share capital of the Company on a standalone basis.
The buyback is proposed from all equity shareholders as on a record date to be announced later, on a proportionate basis through the tender offer route.

The Buyback Size does not exceed 25% of the aggregate of the paid-up capital and free reserves, based on the latest audited interim condensed standalone and consolidated financial statements as on June 30, 2025.
The buyback has not yet been approved by the Company's shareholders and has not yet commenced.
key_facts.section_heading: Additional information pursuant to U.S. Law

key_facts.fact: Infosys will seek and obtain exemptive relief from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on certain aspects of the tender offer procedures due to conflicting regulatory requirements between Indian and U.S. laws.
ADS holders may participate in the buyback if they cancel ADSs and withdraw the underlying Equity Shares to become equity shareholders as on the Record Date.

corporate_actions.description: Infosys approved a proposal to buy back up to 10,00,00,000 fully paid-up equity shares of face value ₹ 5/- each, representing up to 2.41% of existing total paid-up equity share capital on a standalone basis, through the tender offer route.
corporate_actions.amount_or_ratio: ₹ 18,000 Crore; ₹ 1,800/- per Equity Share; up to 2.41%

risk_factors.risk: The buyback has not yet been approved by shareholders and has not yet commenced.
risk_factors.potential_impact: Any offer to purchase or solicitation of offers to sell will be made only if the Buyback is approved by shareholders.
risk_factors.section_heading: Additional information pursuant to U.S. Law

risk_factors.risk: Infosys needs exemptive relief from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission for certain tender offer procedures due to conflicting Indian and U.S. regulatory requirements.

processing_notes: The document includes a corrupted sentence around approval by remote e-voting; only the clearly stated shareholder approval condition was extracted. Pre-buyback shareholding pattern was not fully extracted because the main investor-relevant event is the buyback, though the aggregate paid-up share count and buyback percentage are captured.
